anti_patterns.name: string_concatenation_loop
anti_patterns.description: String concatenation in a loop creates a new string object each iteration. Use StringBuilder for repeated concatenation.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect += on string variables inside for/foreach/while loops.
anti_patterns.fix: Use StringBuilder and call .Append() inside the loop, then .ToString() after.

anti_patterns.name: empty_catch_rethrow
anti_patterns.description: catch { throw; } is pointless. catch (Exception ex) { throw ex; } loses the stack trace. Use throw; not throw ex;
anti_patterns.detection: Detect catch blocks that only contain throw ex; (losing stack trace).
anti_patterns.fix: Use 'throw;' to preserve the original stack trace, or remove the try/catch entirely if no handling is needed.

anti_patterns.name: god_class
anti_patterns.description: Classes with too many methods or responsibilities should be decomposed. A class with 10+ public methods likely violates single responsibility.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect classes with excessive method count.
anti_patterns.fix: Split into focused classes. Extract related methods into their own service/handler classes.

anti_patterns.name: magic_strings
anti_patterns.description: String literals used for comparison, dictionary keys, or routing are fragile. Use constants, enums, or nameof().
anti_patterns.detection: Detect string literals in if comparisons or dictionary access.
anti_patterns.fix: Extract to const fields, use nameof() for property/method names, or use enums.

anti_patterns.name: linq_misuse
anti_patterns.description: Inefficient LINQ patterns: chained .Where(), .Count() > 0 instead of .Any(), .ToList() mid-chain.
anti_patterns.fix: Combine Where predicates, use .Any() instead of .Count() > 0, defer .ToList() to the end.

anti_patterns.name: new_list_add_range
anti_patterns.description: Creating a new List then immediately AddRange. Use collection initializer or LINQ ToList().
anti_patterns.detection: Detect new List<T>() followed by AddRange.

anti_patterns.name: god_class_csharp
anti_patterns.description: Class with too many responsibilities. Hard to test, maintain, and reason about.
anti_patterns.fix: Split into smaller focused classes. Apply single responsibility principle.
